title: Post-transplant extramedullary relapse of NPM1-mutated AML in a 24-year-old female

patient.chief_complaint: New skin changes on the back and extremities
patient.vital_signs: BP 120/75 mmHg, HR 116/min, T° 36.8 °C, respiratory rate 16/min.

1.capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient with new symptoms in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine blood panels, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging as first-line tests. Invasive tissue sampling and specialized analyses usually require additional coordination and are not available in this round.

2.capabilities: The patient is now admitted for further evaluation. You can organize targeted tissue sampling and a broader diagnostic workup, including advanced imaging and initial pathology assessments. Some specialized analyses may still be pending or require referral to external laboratories. They will be available in the next round.

3.setting: Inpatient Ward / Multidisciplinary Tumor Board (Definitive Characterization)
3.capabilities: You now have access to results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including detailed pathology, molecular profiling, and measurable residual disease assessments, as well as a complete overview of prior therapies. You are expected to integrate these data into a definitive diagnosis, risk assessment, and treatment strategy.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics or background characteristics such as age, sex, performance status, comorbidities, social situation, or anthropometrics.
demographics.content:
Age: 24
Sex: Female
Performance status: ECOG 0
Comorbidities: Psychiatric disturbance, history of alloSCT
Social history: Lives with her partner and one 2-year-old child. Not currently employed; she had begun law studies but paused due to illness.
Height: 164 cm; Bodyweight: 62 kg.

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, or current outpatient medication.
history_physical.content:
On June 4, 2025, the patient presented with new skin changes, first noticed by her partner on the back and now also on the extremities. She reported intermittent headaches and mild nausea, as well as chronic joint pain and stiffness. Otherwise, she was in good general condition, afebrile, and showed no signs of skin or mucosal GvHD. A marked psychological stress reaction was noted.
Current medication: pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IE 1×/week, calcium 500 mg 1-0-0, posaconazole 300 mg 1×/d, ruxolitinib 10 mg 1-0-1, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg orally once daily, three times per week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday), steroid cream p.r.n.

cbc.valid_request_if: Requests for a complete blood count, including hemoglobin, white blood cell count, platelets, red cell indices, and differential.
cbc.content: CBC: Hb 14.2 g/dL, WBC 6.8 ×10⁹/L, Plt 240 ×10⁹/L; MCV 89 fL, MCH 30 pg; differential: neutrophils 58%, lymphocytes 32%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.

coagulation.valid_request_if: Requests for coagulation parameters such as PT/INR, aPTT, fibrinogen, or D-dimer.
coagulation.content: Coagulation: aPTT 30 sec, INR 1.0, fibrinogen 3.0 g/L, D-dimer <0.2 mg/L.

chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for a chemistry/metabolic panel, including electrolytes, renal and hepatic function tests, and cardiac biomarkers.
chemistry.content: Chemistry: Na 140 mmol/L, K 4.2 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, BUN 14 mg/dL, AST 22 U/L, ALT 24 U/L, bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL, LDH 180 U/L, uric acid 5.0 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.35 mmol/L, NT-proBNP 1250 pg/mL, Troponin T 0.024 ng/mL.

ultrasound_abdomen.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound, sonography of the kidneys or urinary tract, or non-cardiac abdominal imaging.
ultrasound_abdomen.content: Abdominal ultrasound (June 5, 2025): Multiple rounded lesions (2–3 cm) adjacent to the left ureter with associated hydronephrosis grade II. Liver and spleen were normal in size.

echocardiography.valid_request_if: Requests for transthoracic echocardiography or bedside cardiac ultrasound.
echocardiography.content: Echocardiogram: Showed tissue thickening in the pericardium and septal hypertrophy, raising suspicion of an unclear mass lesion.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow aspirate or biopsy findings, including morphology and overall cellularity.
bone_marrow.content: June 5, 2025: Morphology: Normocellular bone marrow with age-appropriate cellularity. Trilineage hematopoiesis present with some dysmorphic maturation in erythroid, myeloid, and megakaryocytic lineages, consistent with reactive changes after alloSCT. No excess blasts or abnormal infiltrates were observed.

bone_marrow_flow.valid_request_if: Requests for flow cytometry or immunophenotyping of the bone marrow.
bone_marrow_flow.content: Flow cytometry: Normal distribution of myeloid cells, mostly immature B cells, with an inverse CD4/CD8 T-cell ratio and normal NK-cell populations. No aberrant antigen expression or increased blast population detected.

skin_biopsy.valid_request_if: Requests for histology or biopsy results of the skin lesions.
skin_biopsy.content: Skin biopsy (2025-06-07): The dermis and subcutis are diffusely infiltrated by atypical hematopoietic cells in a perivascular and interstitial distribution. The cells show irregular nuclear contours, vesicular chromatin, and occasional nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent. Immunohistochemistry: CD34-, CD33+, MPO+, Lysozyme and CD68+, CD117+, negative for CD20-, CD19-, CD3-. Molecular pathology: NPM1 mutation present in cutaneous infiltration.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for conventional cytogenetics or karyotype.

infectious_workup.valid_request_if: Requests for infection screening, inflammatory markers, colonization status, or urinalysis.
infectious_workup.content: 2025-06-10: Infection screen: CRP 8.6 mg/dL, PCT <0.1 ng/mL. Nasopharyngeal swab negative. Rectal/skin swabs: ESBL-producing E. coli; VRE positive, MRSA negative. Urinalysis showed leukocyturia (>500/µL), positive nitrite, and bacteria (+++). Ciprofloxacin 500 mg 1-0-1 was initiated.

ultrasound_followup.valid_request_if: Requests for repeat abdominal ultrasound or reassessment of hydronephrosis.
ultrasound_followup.content: 2025-06-10: A second ultrasound was performed, showing left-sided hydronephrosis grade I–II.

lumbar_puncture.valid_request_if: Requests for lumbar puncture results, cerebrospinal fluid analysis, or CSF cytology.
lumbar_puncture.content: Lumbar puncture (2025-06-06): CSF analysis revealed mild pleocytosis (12 cells/µL, predominantly lymphocytes) with normal glucose and protein levels. Cytology showed no leukemic blasts; flow cytometry was not performed.

pet_ct.valid_request_if: Requests for FDG-PET/CT or whole-body metabolic imaging.
pet_ct.content: FDG-PET/CT (2025-06-07): Revealed multiple FDG-avid cutaneous lesions (SUVmax 6.2) and intra-abdominal lesions adjacent to the left ureter (SUVmax 8.1), accompanied by several mildly FDG-avid mediastinal lymph nodes (SUVmax 4.5).

cardiac_mri.valid_request_if: Requests for cardiac MRI or detailed cross-sectional imaging of the heart and pericardium.
cardiac_mri.content: Cardiac MRI: Demonstrated contrast-enhancing pericardial tissue adjacent to the left ventricle, with septal thickening.

brain_mri.valid_request_if: Requests for brain MRI, neuroimaging, or imaging of the meninges.

molecular_panel_bm.valid_request_if: Requests for comprehensive molecular testing or NGS of the bone marrow.
molecular_panel_bm.content: Bone marrow: NPM1 negative, FLT3-ITD/TKD negative, IDH1/2 negative, CEBPA negative. RUNX1–RUNX1T1 negative. AML-MR–associated genes (ASXL1, BCOR, EZH2, RUNX1, SF3B1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2) negative. Other recurrent AML-associated mutations (TP53, DNMT3A, TET2, WT1, NRAS, KRAS, KIT, PTPN11, CBFB-MYH11, KMT2A rearrangements, inv(3)/MECOM, NUP98 fusions) not detected.

molecular_panel_skin.valid_request_if: Requests for molecular testing of the extramedullary lesions or confirmation of mutation status in the skin sample.
molecular_panel_skin.content: Skin sample: NPM1 mutation detected in the extramedullary infiltration.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease status, including flow cytometry, molecular MRD, or chimerism.
mrd_status.content: Flow cytrometry: MRD negative in bone marrow. NPM1 in bone marrow negative. Chimerism: <0.06% recipient.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior diagnosis, induction and consolidation regimens, salvage therapies, transplant details, and prior complications.
treatment_history.content:
Diagnosis: 2024-02-21, AML with NPM1 mutation (WHO 2022). ELN 2022 risk: favorable.
Initial immunophenotype (February 21, 2024): CD34^low^ blasts expressing CD33, CD14, CD64, CD117, MPO, and CD13, negative for HLA-DR, CD7, CD56, and TdT.
Induction (Cycle 1): “7+3” (cytarabine 200 mg/m² for 7 days (2,352 mg total) + daunorubicin 60 mg/m² for 3 days (303 mg total)) plus gemtuzumab ozogamicin 5 mg on days 1, 4, and 7 (15 mg total). Complications: Neutropenic fever and pneumonia, otherwise well tolerated.
Consolidation (March–July 2024): Four cycles of high-dose cytarabine (3 g/m² BID for 3 days = 30,240 mg total per cycle). Response: Complete remission (CR). MRD negativity in bone marrow (flow cytometry and NPM1 PCR) achieved after the first consolidation cycle.
September 14, 2024: Patient noted palpable swellings in the chest. Gynecologic evaluation showed bead-like lymph nodes on ultrasound. Biopsy confirmed AML infiltration. Repeated bone marrow biopsy demonstrated ongoing CR but increased NPM1 levels (ratio 1.42). PET-CT was not performed. Donor search initiated.
Salvage therapy (September 20, 2024): Venetoclax 400 mg days 1–28 plus azacitidine 75 mg/m² days 1–7. September 28, 2024: Hospitalization due to neutropenic fever. Meropenem 1 g TID was initiated for 7 days (21 g total). Posaconazole 300 mg/day was started on day 13 of cycle 1, and venetoclax was reduced to 70 mg for the last 14 days (total dose 6,180 mg). Patient was discharged on October 8, 2024. After one cycle, extramedullary lesions were no longer palpable. Neutrophils recovered on October 18, 2024. PET-CT confirmed no further delineable lesions.
Second cycle (November 2, 2024): Venetoclax 70 mg days 1–14 (due to comedication with posaconazole 300 mg), achieving complete remission.
Allogeneic SCT (December 7, 2024): Haploidentical sibling donor; conditioning with fludarabine 30 mg/m² for 5 days, treosulfan 14 g/m² for 3 days, and thiotepa 5 mg/kg twice on day -2. GVHD prophylaxis: Post-transplant cyclophosphamide (50 mg/kg on days +3 and +4), mycophenolate mofetil (MMF), and tacrolimus.
Complications: Febrile episodes with suspected central venous catheter infection. Empiric antibiotic therapy was initiated with piperacillin/tazobactam 4.5 g TID for 3 days, later escalated to meropenem 1 g i.v. TID for 13 days, and vancomycin 1 g BID (later increased to 1.5 g-0-1.25 g for 5 days) for Gram-positive coverage. Hemolysis was suspected (haptoglobin <10 mg/dL, schistocytes 6‰ on peripheral smear, and LDH 324 U/L, bilirubin 1.2 mg/dL) in the setting of ABO-incompatible transplantation. The patient received rituximab 375 mg/m² i.v. weekly × 4 doses (total 2,520 mg).
January 10, 2025: Complete remission (CR); 100% donor chimerism; no MRD detectable in bone marrow.
March 28, 2025 (day +100): PET-CT showed no FDG-avid lesions. Day +107: Patient developed skin GvHD grade III°, steroid-refractory (Days 1–7: prednisolone 120 mg daily, tapered by 10 mg every 3 days) but responsive to ruxolitinib 10 mg BID. GvHD overlapped into mild to moderate chronic GvHD, controlled with ruxolitinib 10 mg BID.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise summary of the current disease status or relapse pattern.
disease_status.content: Relapse type: Early post-transplant extramedullary relapse of NPM1+ AML.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for special considerations relevant to treatment planning, including organ involvement, prior toxicities, immune status, and psychosocial context.
special_considerations.content: Exclusive extramedullary AML relapse. CNS involvement was suspected but cerebrospinal fluid analysis showed no blasts. Additional cardiac involvement is suspected, with elevated myocardial markers. Abdominal lesions resulted in hydronephrosis grade II. The prior course was complicated by acute GVHD grade III° and subsequent chronic GVHD, making immune escape a plausible mechanism. Performance status remained good; however, the patient was under marked psychological stress, expressing reluctance to undergo another inpatient admission due to having a young child at home. Significant stress reaction noted at the time of diagnosis disclosure; crisis intervention team involved.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Relapse or secondary malignancy in post-alloSCT patient

treatment_plan.action: CT scan with contrast or PET-CT for staging of lesions
Detailed labs including CRP, PCT, infectious disease screening, lymphocyte subpopulations, CMV/EBV PCR, unsorted chimerism, and rheumatism diagnostics
MRI brain followed by lumbar puncture for headaches and nausea
TEE and cardiac MRI for pericardial/septal abnormalities
Psychiatric consult for marked psychological stress reaction

key_findings_quotes: new skin changes, first noticed by her partner on the back and now also on the extremities
intermittent headaches and mild nausea, as well as chronic joint pain and stiffness
Multiple rounded lesions (2–3 cm) adjacent to the left ureter with associated hydronephrosis grade II
thickening in the pericardium and septal hypertrophy, raising suspicion of an unclear mass lesion

round_2.working_diagnosis: Extramedullary (relapse of) AML with NPM1 mutation
differentials: Secondary malignancy in retroperitoneum and pericardium

treatment_plan.action: Molecular genetic panel for AML in both skin and bone marrow
Cytoreduction with hydroxyurea until molecular genetics are available, especially for targetable mutations
Taper ruxolitinib to enhance graft-versus-leukemia effect
Consider further histology of pericardial or retroperitoneal mass
Treat UTI with appropriate antibiotics considering VRE colonization status

key_findings_quotes: CD34-, CD33+, MPO+, Lysozyme and CD68+, CD117+, negative for CD20-, CD19-, CD3-
Molecular pathology: NPM1 Mutation present in cutaneous infiltration
multiple FDG-avid cutaneous lesions (SUVmax 6.2) and intra-abdominal lesions adjacent to the left ureter (SUVmax 8.1)
positive nitrite, and bacteria (+++); VRE positive

round_3.working_diagnosis: Post-transplant extramedullary relapse of NPM1-mutated AML
differentials: CNS involvement suspected but not confirmed

treatment_plan.action: Request insurance or compassionate use approval for menin inhibitor combined with hypomethylating agent (azacitidine or decitabine/cedazuridine) and venetoclax
Repeat CSF analysis to rule out CNS involvement; if detected, initiate intrathecal therapy (cytarabine plus dexamethasone) until CSF is cleared
Plan second allogeneic SCT from unrelated donor with conditioning containing 8-12 Gy TBI due to extramedullary AML
Acceptable alternative: Salvage chemotherapy with FLAG-IDA plus venetoclax followed by second alloSCT
Provide psychological support and informed consent discussion considering patient's reluctance for re-admission

key_findings_quotes: Early post-transplant extramedullary relapse of NPM1+ AML
NPM1 mutation in skin but not bone marrow; Flow cytometry: MRD negative in bone marrow
Exclusive extramedullary AML relapse. CNS involvement was suspected but cerebrospinal fluid analysis showed no blasts
prior course was complicated by acute GVHD grade III and subsequent chronic GVHD, making immune escape a plausible mechanism
7+3 plus gemtuzumab ozogamicin, four cycles of high-dose cytarabine, Venetoclax plus azacitidine
Haploidentical sibling donor; marked psychological stress, expressing reluctance to undergo another inpatient admission
